rawContent: {"stage":"build","insights":[{"kind":"convention","text":"Use withAuth HOC at route level rather than manual if(!user) guards inside handler bodies; guards become dead code once the HOC is applied.","scope":"src/auth/withAuth.ts"},{"kind":"gotcha","text":"Auth service and JWT verification error messages (e.g. 'jwt expired', 'User not found', 'Token revoked') leak internal details in 401 responses and must be sanitized to generic messages before product","scope":"src/auth/withAuth.ts"},{"kind":"decision","text":"Apply withAuth with optional:true to /api/my-route — optional:true permits unauthenticated access but still attaches a user if a valid token is present, useful for routes that behave differently based","scope":"/api/my-route"},{"kind":"lesson","text":"Protected route test strategy: verify both unauthenticated requests (missing/invalid token) and malformed token payloads receive 401, not just one error case.","scope":"integration tests"}]}
